title: Medium Priority Integrations
impact: Medium - Extends TaskScheduler integration to ArchiveManager, SemanticSearch, and TransactionManager

archiveProgress.current: No progress reporting during archive operations
archiveProgress.target: Progress callback for entity evaluation and archival phases

semanticSearchCancellation.current: indexAll has onProgress but no cancellation

tasks.title: Enhance ArchiveManager with Progress and Cancellation
tasks.description: Add progress callback and cancellation support to ArchiveManager.archiveEntities() method using the LongRunningOperationOptions interface.

implementation.purpose: Enable progress tracking and cancellation during entity archival, improving UX for large archive operations.
keyDecisions: Extend existing ArchiveOptions interface with LongRunningOperationOptions fields
Report two-phase progress: evaluation (0-50%) and archival (50-100%)
Support cancellation between phases and during evaluation
Keep original return type ArchiveResult (NOT CancellableResult wrapper)

currentCode.signature: async archiveEntities(criteria: ArchiveCriteria, options: ArchiveOptions | boolean = {}): Promise<ArchiveResult>

stepByStep.details: Import checkCancellation, createProgressReporter, createProgress from utils/index.js
Add onProgress?: ProgressCallback and signal?: AbortSignal to the interface

stepByStep.action: Add progress reporting during entity evaluation phase (0-50%)
stepByStep.details: Report progress every 50 entities during the evaluation loop
Report progress at 75% after saveToArchive, 100% after completion

stepByStep.action: Add cancellation check before archive write
Run tests and add new test cases
stepByStep.details: Verify backward compatibility, add progress/cancellation tests

acceptanceCriteria: ArchiveOptions extended with onProgress and signal fields
Progress callback receives updates during evaluation (0-50%) and archival (50-100%)
AbortSignal cancellation throws OperationCancelledError at safe points
Legacy calls with boolean parameter still work
All existing archive tests pass (backward compatible)

tasks.title: Add AbortSignal Support to SemanticSearch.indexAll()
tasks.description: Enhance SemanticSearch.indexAll() to support AbortSignal cancellation. The method already has onProgress callback via SemanticIndexOptions, so only cancellation support is needed.

implementation.purpose: Enable cancellation of semantic indexing operations, which can be slow for large graphs due to embedding API calls.
keyDecisions: Check cancellation between batches in indexAll
Keep original return type (no CancellableResult wrapper)

currentCode.signature: async indexAll(graph: ReadonlyKnowledgeGraph, options: SemanticIndexOptions = {}): Promise<{ indexed: number; skipped: number; errors: number }>

stepByStep.details: Add signal?: AbortSignal field to the interface

stepByStep.action: Add cancellation check at start of indexAll
Add cancellation check in batch processing loop
stepByStep.details: At the start of each batch iteration, check for cancellation
Test that aborting signal throws OperationCancelledError

acceptanceCriteria: All existing semantic search tests pass

tasks.title: Add Progress Callback to TransactionManager.commit()
tasks.description: Add progress callback support to TransactionManager.commit() method. Note: Cancellation is intentionally NOT supported to maintain atomic transaction semantics.

implementation.purpose: Enable progress tracking during transaction commits, showing operation-by-operation progress for large transactions.
keyDecisions: Create CommitOptions interface with onProgress?: ProgressCallback only
Do NOT support cancellation (atomic semantics - use rollback() before commit if needed)
Report progress after each operation in commit()
implementation.atomicityNote: Transactions must be atomic - either all operations succeed or all fail. Cancellation during commit would violate atomicity. Users should call rollback() before commit if they want to cancel.

stepByStep.details: interface CommitOptions { onProgress?: ProgressCallback; } - Note: NO signal field
Report 0% at start with message 'Starting transaction'

stepByStep.action: Add progress reporting in operation loop
stepByStep.details: After each applyOperation call, report progress with operation type
Report 100% after save with message 'Transaction committed'
Test that progress callback receives calls for each operation

acceptanceCriteria: CommitOptions interface exists with onProgress callback (NO signal)
Progress callback receives updates after each operation
Progress includes operation type in currentTaskId
Documentation clearly states no cancellation support (by design)

successCriteria: All three tasks completed with passing tests
TransactionManager.commit supports progress callback (no cancellation by design)
notes: Sprint 2 depends on Sprint 1 Task 9B.1.1 for operationUtils
TransactionManager intentionally does NOT support cancellation during commit (atomic semantics)
SemanticSearch already has onProgress, just needs signal added
ArchiveManager progress should reflect both evaluation and archival phases
Use the existing ProgressCallback type from taskScheduler.ts
